Add shared mock functions for all context modules

Add useFilteredList mock for DialogCommandPalette
Fix JSX importSource to match SolidJS JSX runtime at solid-js/h/jsx-runtime
Fix JSX transform mode for bun test compatibility with SolidJS
Rewrite happydom.ts with proper solid-js mocks
diff_snapshot:
diff --git a/packages/app/happydom.ts b/packages/app/happydom.ts
index de726718f..7fafe2b2a 100644
--- a/packages/app/happydom.ts
+++ b/packages/app/happydom.ts
@@ -1,9 +1,26 @@
+// Stub to override solid-js module resolution in tests when bun resolves to server version.
+// This file is loaded before tests and re-maps solid-js to the client dist.
+// Used in combination with bun's module mocking.
+
 import { GlobalRegistrator } from "@happy-dom/global-registrator"
+import { mock } from "bun:test"
 
 GlobalRegistrator.register()
 
+// Force solid-js to resolve to the client build so render() works in tests.
+mock.module("solid-js", () => {
+  // Using require() since this runs before ESM imports are resolved.
+  // solid-js is a combination of ESM exports; the CJS build re-exports everything
+  // as named exports under `module.exports`.
+  return require("solid-js/dist/solid.cjs")

reason: Revert tsconfig JSX settings to original
Revert bunfig JSX importSource to original
Set React as global for JSX transform compatibility
Use ESM async imports in mock factories to avoid CJS bypass issue
diff_snapshot:
diff --git a/packages/app/happydom.ts b/packages/app/happydom.ts
index de726718f..7c3898234 100644
--- a/packages/app/happydom.ts
+++ b/packages/app/happydom.ts
@@ -1,9 +1,41 @@
+// Stub to override solid-js module resolution in tests when bun resolves to server version.
+// This file is loaded before tests and re-maps solid-js to the client dist.
+// Used in combination with bun's module mocking.
+
 import { GlobalRegistrator } from "@happy-dom/global-registrator"
+import { mock } from "bun:test"
 
 GlobalRegistrator.register()
 
+// Force solid-js and its sub-modules to resolve to the client build.
+// We use async factory functions so bun resolves via ESM (which respects mocks),
+// avoiding CJS require() chains that bypass the mock system.
+mock.module("solid-js", async () => {
+  // @ts-expect-error - dynamic import handled by bun's bundler
+  return await import("solid-js/dist/solid.js")

reason: Complete rewrite using async ESM-based mock system
diff_snapshot:
diff --git a/packages/app/happydom.ts b/packages/app/happydom.ts
index de726718f..a8458351d 100644
--- a/packages/app/happydom.ts
+++ b/packages/app/happydom.ts
@@ -1,9 +1,46 @@
+// Stub to override solid-js module resolution in tests when bun resolves to server version.
+// This file is loaded before tests and re-maps solid-js to the client dist.
+// Used in combination with bun's module mocking.
+
 import { GlobalRegistrator } from "@happy-dom/global-registrator"
+import { mock } from "bun:test"
 
 GlobalRegistrator.register()
 
+// SolidJS package.json uses conditional exports with `node` condition defaulting to
+// the server build. Since bun test sets the `node` condition, imports resolve to
+// server modules where client-only functions (render, insert, spread, etc.) throw.
+//
+// We mock all solid-js sub-modules to force the browser/client builds.
+// Using `mock.module` with async factories ensures bun resolves through ESM

reason: Use pre-loaded ESM modules for SolidJS client builds
diff_snapshot:
diff --git a/packages/app/happydom.ts b/packages/app/happydom.ts
index de726718f..b8bfcf989 100644
--- a/packages/app/happydom.ts
+++ b/packages/app/happydom.ts
@@ -1,9 +1,48 @@
+// Stub to override solid-js module resolution in tests when bun resolves to server version.
+// This file is loaded before tests and re-maps solid-js to the client dist.
+// Used in combination with bun's module mocking.
+
 import { GlobalRegistrator } from "@happy-dom/global-registrator"
 
 GlobalRegistrator.register()
 
+// ── SolidJS module resolution fix ───────────────────────────────────────
+// bun test resolves solid-js to the server build (because bun sets the `node`
+// export condition). We need the client build for DOM operations.
+//
+// Strategy: Use `import()` (ESM) to load the client builds and then register
+// them with mock.module() so subsequent imports from test files use the client build.
+// We mock at the exact specifiers that dialog components use.
